content.content:
110	- **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.
111	- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.
112	- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).

726	- **Learnings for future iterations:**
727	  - **Run `bun run scripts/test/probe-jwt-drift.ts` at the top of any iteration that plans to touch a UI screen doing direct PostgREST reads** (anything under `(creator)/` or `(lister)/` that calls `.from('x').select()` with the cached token). content.text: Now I'll insert two new patterns at the end of the Codebase Patterns section, then append the US-042 progress entry.

input.old_string:
- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).

---
input.new_string:
- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).
- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.
- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.

---

lines: - **Concurrency via version-id pin + FOR UPDATE re-read, not engine duplication in PL/pgSQL.** When an edge function needs to "re-evaluate eligibility against the NOW-current listing version" (spec §5.5) without duplicating the TS eligibility engine in SQL, the sanctioned split is: (1) edge fn reads `listings.current_version_id` and runs TS eligibility against it, then (2) calls an RPC that re-reads the same listing under `select ... for update`, compares the locked `current_version_id` to `p_expected_version_id`, and returns `LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED` on mismatch so the client re-opens the detail view. The row lock serializes this flow against `app_private.bump_listing_version` (which runs under its own row lock on the listings UPDATE). Invariant depends on the bump trigger producing a new `current_version_id` for every eligibility-affecting mutation — if a future trigger change lets a condition edit land without bumping, the RPC would silently commit against stale TS eligibility. Document the dependency in the RPC header. Ref: `supabase/migrations/20260417270000_us_041_apply_to_listing_rpc.sql` + `apply-to-listing/index.ts`.

- **Fail-closed applies to malformed data, not just missing data.** Spec §3d's fail-closed rule covers the "null actual metric" case (user hasn't been scraped), but the same principle extends to unreadable THRESHOLD values: a `gte` pre-condition with a NULL or non-numeric `numeric_threshold` must fail the check, not silently pass. Silently returning `null` from the single-condition evaluator for a malformed row is how a future mis-migrated condition flips a listing to "always eligible" without any alarm. Rule: every branch of an eligibility-or-permission evaluator that hits unreadable input returns a fail, never an early "skip." Schema CHECKs should prevent the malformed row from existing, but the evaluator is the last line of defense. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` `evaluatePreCondition`.

- **Extract shared modules under `supabase/functions/_shared/` as soon as a second function needs the same logic.** US-039's eligibility engine was inline; US-041 needed the same engine for its server-side re-check. Pattern: extract to `_shared/<name>.ts` with a header comment naming the consumers and the spec sections it implements, have BOTH functions import it via `../_shared/<name>.ts`, and **redeploy every consumer function** in the same iteration — the deployed bundle of a prior function still contains the inlined copy, so a future redeploy of that function without the shared file would fail with `Module not found`. The `deploy_edge_function` `files` array must include every `_shared/*.ts` dependency, not just the new one. Ref: `_shared/eligibility.ts` shared by `get-listing-detail` (v4) + `apply-to-listing` (v2).

+- **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is unreliable inside an RN `<Modal>` (and therefore inside our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens its own native window, so KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame disagree and the contents do not get pushed up. Replace KAV with a direct `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow' / 'keyboardWillHide')` (use `keyboardDidShow`/`keyboardDidHide` on Android) that drives `paddingBottom` on the inner `<View>` from `e.endCoordinates.height`. Remember that `BottomSheet` snap point must accommodate `keyboard_height (~336px iOS) + content_height` — for the apply sheet that meant raising `snapPoints` from `[0.7]` to `[0.85]`. Ref: `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx`.

+- **Defer `router.replace`/`router.push` after dismissing a `BottomSheet` by ~350ms** so the sheet's spring-out animation (`SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms — see `src/design/motion.ts`) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts. Without the delay, the sheet slides down on top of the freshly-rendered destination, which looks broken. The same delay applies to any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path. Ref: `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` `onApplied`.

## 2026-04-17 24:35 — US-042
- New `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx` (~340 lines): bottom sheet rendered at snap point 0.85, header copy "You're applying to" + listing title, optional pitch `<TextInput>` capped at 280 chars (per story AC; design.md mock shows 0/160 — Spec gap noted in US-042 prd notes), live `pitch.length/280` counter, "By applying you confirm you'll follow the campaign rules." disclaimer, "Send application" CTA. Submit calls `supabase.functions.invoke('apply-to-listing', { body: { listing_id, cover_note? } })` and decodes `FunctionsHttpError` via `(error.context as Response).json()` (Codebase Pattern #98) to map the 5 structured error codes — INELIGIBLE 403 / LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED 409 / ALREADY_APPLIED 409 / LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE 409 / LISTING_NOT_FOUND 404 — each to a one-line error toast plus an `onServerStateChanged` callback so the parent reloads the listing detail and the eligibility rail/CTA reflect the new server reality.
- `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` wired up: replaces the US-040 stub apply toast with real `ApplySheet` mount + open/close state, and on `onApplied` shows a success toast and `setTimeout(350)` → `router.replace('/(creator)/applications')` so the sheet's spring-out animation (~300ms) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts.
- Two scope/spec divergences from the story AC, documented in the file headers + US-042 prd notes:
  1. Field is `cover_note` (canonical per `docs/tech-architecture.md §5.5` + `applications.cover_note` column) — story AC says `pitch`. UI label keeps "Pitch (optional)" for the user; the wire field is `cover_note`. Same call as US-041.
  2. Server returns the structured codes above (per spec §5.5), not the AC's `422 {reasons}`. Each code maps to its own toast + reload behavior so the spec contract is honored at the API boundary.
- Submit hardening: `useRef`-based synchronous lock guards against rapid double-taps (Codebase Pattern #106). `KeyboardAvoidingView` swapped for direct `Keyboard.addListener` driving `paddingBottom` on the inner View — KAV is unreliable inside RN `<Modal>` because the modal opens its own window and KAV's measured frame disagrees with the keyboard's reported frame (new Codebase Pattern). Snap raised from `[0.7]` → `[0.85]` to fit content + ~336px iOS keyboard + CTA.
- Files changed:
  - `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx` (new, ~340 lines)
  - `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` (wired ApplySheet + setTimeout-guarded router.replace; removed US-040 stub toast)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-empty.png` (new — sheet open, no pitch typed)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-pitch.png` (new — sheet open with pitch text)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-with-keyboard.png` (new — sheet at 0.85 with keyboard up, CTA still visible above keyboard)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-submitted.png` (new — Applied tab post-submit)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema changes) / mobile-mcp ✓ (4 screenshots on iPhone 17 Pro sim) / DB row 74e23751-1698-4475-aefe-fd09e0cb1fbd verified inserted with `status='pending'`, `cover_note='Quick pitch after refactor.'`
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) hardcoded 336px iOS keyboard fallback removed in favor of trusting the `Keyboard` event (the listener fires reliably post-mount, fallback was a stale workaround from the first verification attempt); (2) stale-closure on `submitting` swapped for `useRef` lock per Pattern #106; (3) `router.replace` wrapped in `setTimeout(350)` so the BottomSheet spring-out plays before the destination mounts; (4) `already_applied` toast variant changed from `'info'` → `'error'` for taxonomy consistency with the other 4 server failure cases; (5) `maxLength={COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN + 40}` collapsed to exact `COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN` and the resulting dead `overLimit`/`inputWrapError` style + `remaining` countdown removed.
- Spec gap: design.md §4.3 mock shows the counter as `0/160`, but the story AC says 280 chars. Used 280 (story wins for a numeric AC; design mock is illustrative). Resolution: when the design system docs get a real "form copy" inventory pass, reconcile the cap there.
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is the wrong tool inside any `<Modal>`-based component (which includes our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens a separate native window; KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame live in different coordinate spaces, so the sheet content does not get pushed up. Use `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow'/'keyboardWillHide')` to drive `paddingBottom` on the inner View. Cost is one extra `useEffect` and one piece of state. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.
  - **Sheet snap-point math must include the keyboard.** A 0.7-snap sheet (596px on iPhone 17 Pro) + 336px iOS keyboard + ~300px form content overflowed by 66px and clipped the CTA. Bumped to 0.85 (724px). Rule: any sheet that contains a focused TextInput needs `snap_point * screen_height >= keyboard_height + content_height + safe_area`. For iPhone 17 Pro that's roughly `0.85` for a one-input form.
  - **Reanimated `translateY` does not update the React Native accessibility frame.** During mobile-mcp verification, `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` reported the Send button at its un-translated y-coordinate even though screenshots showed it visibly translated. Workaround: trust the screenshot for visual correctness and use `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` against the on-screen position rather than the a11y-reported position. Don't waste a cycle "debugging" the gap — it is by design (Reanimated runs in the UI thread and does not push frame updates back to a11y).
  - **Defer `router.replace` after closing a BottomSheet by ~350ms.** Without the delay, the sheet's spring-out animation plays on top of the destination screen and looks broken. `SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms; 350ms gives a small safety margin. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. Same rule applies for any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path.
  - **When the AC and the spec disagree at the wire (field name, error code shape), the spec wins at the API boundary and the UI keeps the user-facing label from the AC.** US-042 AC: `pitch`; spec: `cover_note`. UI label "Pitch (optional)" stays; wire field is `cover_note`. Same precedent as US-041. The "Spec gap" note in prd.json captures the discrepancy so the next person reading the story doesn't think we ignored the AC.
---

  - **Defer `router.replace` after closing a BottomSheet by ~350ms.** Without the delay, the sheet's spring-out animation plays on top of the destination screen and looks broken. `SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms; 350ms gives a small safety margin. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. Same rule applies for any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path.
  - **When the AC and the spec disagree at the wire (field name, error code shape), the spec wins at the API boundary and the UI keeps the user-facing label from the AC.** US-042 AC: `pitch`; spec: `cover_note`. UI label "Pitch (optional)" stays; wire field is `cover_note`. Same precedent as US-041. The "Spec gap" note in prd.json captures the discrepancy so the next person reading the story doesn't think we ignored the AC.
---

lines: - **`chore(ralph)` commit pattern is appropriate for meta-iterations that produce only notes + diagnostic tooling.** Precedent: commits `5a937a5` (US-033 mark-complete), `a2f0416` (US-031 mark-complete), `b8235e5` (progress-aware watchdog). When a story's code is already committed (as US-036's was in `be06466`) but the iteration can't advance `passes:true` due to upstream env blockers, the chore commit captures the iteration's verification evidence without pretending the story completed.

- **The JWT-drift blocker affects stories that hit PostgREST directly; it does NOT affect stories that route reads through edge functions.** Advance strategy: prefer stories whose data path is `app → edge-function (custom JWT verify) → service-role DB`. US-041 (`apply-to-listing` edge function), US-042 (Apply modal — calls US-041), and most lister-side creation flows (US-049+) follow this shape. Feed + profile + direct-PostgREST-list screens stay blocked until the dashboard fix lands.

+- New `src/screens/listing-detail/ApplySheet.tsx` (~340 lines): bottom sheet rendered at snap point 0.85, header copy "You're applying to" + listing title, optional pitch `<TextInput>` capped at 280 chars (per story AC; design.md mock shows 0/160 — Spec gap noted in US-042 prd notes), live `pitch.length/280` counter, "By applying you confirm you'll follow the campaign rules." disclaimer, "Send application" CTA. Submit calls `supabase.functions.invoke('apply-to-listing', { body: { listing_id, cover_note? } })` and decodes `FunctionsHttpError` via `(error.context as Response).json()` (Codebase Pattern #98) to map the 5 structured error codes — INELIGIBLE 403 / LISTING_VERSION_CHANGED 409 / ALREADY_APPLIED 409 / LISTING_NOT_ACTIVE 409 / LISTING_NOT_FOUND 404 — each to a one-line error toast plus an `onServerStateChanged` callback so the parent reloads the listing detail and the eligibility rail/CTA reflect the new server reality.

+- `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` wired up: replaces the US-040 stub apply toast with real `ApplySheet` mount + open/close state, and on `onApplied` shows a success toast and `setTimeout(350)` → `router.replace('/(creator)/applications')` so the sheet's spring-out animation (~300ms) plays cleanly before the new screen mounts.
+- Two scope/spec divergences from the story AC, documented in the file headers + US-042 prd notes:

+  1. Field is `cover_note` (canonical per `docs/tech-architecture.md §5.5` + `applications.cover_note` column) — story AC says `pitch`. UI label keeps "Pitch (optional)" for the user; the wire field is `cover_note`. Same call as US-041.
+  2. Server returns the structured codes above (per spec §5.5), not the AC's `422 {reasons}`. Each code maps to its own toast + reload behavior so the spec contract is honored at the API boundary.

+- Submit hardening: `useRef`-based synchronous lock guards against rapid double-taps (Codebase Pattern #106). `KeyboardAvoidingView` swapped for direct `Keyboard.addListener` driving `paddingBottom` on the inner View — KAV is unreliable inside RN `<Modal>` because the modal opens its own window and KAV's measured frame disagrees with the keyboard's reported frame (new Codebase Pattern). Snap raised from `[0.7]` → `[0.85]` to fit content + ~336px iOS keyboard + CTA.
+  - `app/(creator)/listing/[id].tsx` (wired ApplySheet + setTimeout-guarded router.replace; removed US-040 stub toast)
+  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-042-sheet-with-keyboard.png` (new — sheet at 0.85 with keyboard up, CTA still visible above keyboard)

+- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors n/a (no schema changes) / mobile-mcp ✓ (4 screenshots on iPhone 17 Pro sim) / DB row 74e23751-1698-4475-aefe-fd09e0cb1fbd verified inserted with `status='pending'`, `cover_note='Quick pitch after refactor.'`

+- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit — (1) hardcoded 336px iOS keyboard fallback removed in favor of trusting the `Keyboard` event (the listener fires reliably post-mount, fallback was a stale workaround from the first verification attempt); (2) stale-closure on `submitting` swapped for `useRef` lock per Pattern #106; (3) `router.replace` wrapped in `setTimeout(350)` so the BottomSheet spring-out plays before the destination mounts; (4) `already_applied` toast variant changed from `'info'` → `'error'` for taxonomy consistency with the other 4 server failure cases; (5) `maxLength={COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN + 40}` collapsed to exact `COVER_NOTE_MAX_LEN` and the resulting dead `overLimit`/`inputWrapError` style + `remaining` countdown removed.

+- Spec gap: design.md §4.3 mock shows the counter as `0/160`, but the story AC says 280 chars. Used 280 (story wins for a numeric AC; design mock is illustrative). Resolution: when the design system docs get a real "form copy" inventory pass, reconcile the cap there.

+  - **`KeyboardAvoidingView` is the wrong tool inside any `<Modal>`-based component (which includes our `BottomSheet`).** The Modal opens a separate native window; KAV's measured frame and the keyboard's reported frame live in different coordinate spaces, so the sheet content does not get pushed up. Use `Keyboard.addListener('keyboardWillShow'/'keyboardWillHide')` to drive `paddingBottom` on the inner View. Cost is one extra `useEffect` and one piece of state. Promoted to Codebase Patterns.

+  - **Sheet snap-point math must include the keyboard.** A 0.7-snap sheet (596px on iPhone 17 Pro) + 336px iOS keyboard + ~300px form content overflowed by 66px and clipped the CTA. Bumped to 0.85 (724px). Rule: any sheet that contains a focused TextInput needs `snap_point * screen_height >= keyboard_height + content_height + safe_area`. For iPhone 17 Pro that's roughly `0.85` for a one-input form.

+  - **Reanimated `translateY` does not update the React Native accessibility frame.** During mobile-mcp verification, `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` reported the Send button at its un-translated y-coordinate even though screenshots showed it visibly translated. Workaround: trust the screenshot for visual correctness and use `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` against the on-screen position rather than the a11y-reported position. Don't waste a cycle "debugging" the gap — it is by design (Reanimated runs in the UI thread and does not push frame updates back to a11y).

+  - **Defer `router.replace` after closing a BottomSheet by ~350ms.** Without the delay, the sheet's spring-out animation plays on top of the destination screen and looks broken. `SPRING_SNAPPY` settles in ~300ms; 350ms gives a small safety margin. Promoted to Codebase Patterns. Same rule applies for any imperative navigation triggered from a Modal/BottomSheet success path.

+  - **When the AC and the spec disagree at the wire (field name, error code shape), the spec wins at the API boundary and the UI keeps the user-facing label from the AC.** US-042 AC: `pitch`; spec: `cover_note`. UI label "Pitch (optional)" stays; wire field is `cover_note`. Same precedent as US-041. The "Spec gap" note in prd.json captures the discrepancy so the next person reading the story doesn't think we ignored the AC.
